title: Vinsonia drupacea

The family Annonaceae includes over 230 genera of plants and is commonly known as the custard apple or soursop family. Vinsonia drupacea is one of the lesser-known species within this family. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America.

intro: Vinsonia drupacea, also known as Indian licorice or Prishniparni, is a plant species belonging to the family Celastraceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory infections, due to its numerous health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Vinsonia drupacea include triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Vinsonia drupacea is often prepared as a powder, decoction, or syrup, and is commonly used to treat coughs, colds, and other respiratory ailments.
